title: Events should be raised through a null-conditional invocation
messageFormat: Raise this event with '?.Invoke' so it is not invoked when no handler is attached.
summary: An event with no subscribers is null. Invoking it directly throws a NullReferenceException the moment a handler is absent. A separate if null check before the call is also unsafe, because the last subscriber can unsubscribe on another thread between the check and the invocation.

Raise the event with the null-conditional operator, Handler?.Invoke(...). The compiler captures the delegate into a temporary and calls it only when it is non-null, which is both concise and thread-safe.

seeAlso.text: How to raise base class events
